# Video

https://youtu.be/pS543BQew84

# Explanation

For this project I was originally very inspired by the waves feedback demo found on The Schwartz. I started by taking the code and playing around with it in different ways. I really liked how rotating the previous frame created geometric patterns. It was very psychedlic, reminding me a lot of the tunnels you see while hallucinating on psychedelics. 
My goal was to create something that felt like a couple stages of increasingly trippy tunnels, with some additional effects. I replaced the input for the mouse.y in the waves sin function with a static number, then I created a second sin function to effect the x position (p.x). This created roughly the first pattern in the video, but I made some additional changes. I found that giving that pattern feedback created the image I was interested in, so I created stages using other trig functions, color changing. Lastly I tried changing the strength of the feedback during certain sections, so that it would fade from the tunnels back to the normal wave exchange pattern, but it didn't really work out. I ended up incorporating things that broke if I found them interesting. The black descent into a screen is unintentional, probably due to the calculations creating negative numbers and breaking the shader, but I liked it, and I liked how it came back to the tangent tunnel, like passing out and coming back to a deeper trance.

# Code

[Schwartz](https://charlieroberts.codeberg.page/TheSchwartz/?) [WGSL File](https://github.com/InKremlin/CS-4300/blob/main/LiveCoding.wgsl)
